zotero.abstractNote: We propose Subnetwork Inference, a framework for Bayesian deep learning that performs inference on a subset of a network's weights while keeping the rest fixed. This approach is motivated by the observation that deep neural networks are often over-parameterized and that a small subnetwork can be sufficient to capture the uncertainty of the model. We show that Subnetwork Inference can be applied to a variety of architectures and inference methods, including Laplace approximation and variational inference. Our experiments demonstrate that Subnetwork Inference achieves competitive performance on uncertainty estimation and out-of-distribution detection tasks while being significantly more computationally efficient than full-network inference.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of 38th International Conference on Machine Learning
